docs.title: dafalcon
docs.text: A collection of technical notes, documentation, procedures, and projects.

docs.title: AMS 2 Pro Troubleshooting
docs.text:
We sometimes get weird behavior from the AMS 2 Pro:

  • It holds a filament spool in reverse for several minutes, then errors
  • It "revs" a spool repeatedly with no progress
  • Other errors

Every time it has been caused by a piece of broken filament stuck somewhere in the system.

Here are some troubleshooting steps:

  • Take all the filament out of the AMS and disconnect it from the printer
  • The PFE tubes inside the AMS can be disconnected from the back by depressing the black piece of plasting and pulling the tube. Disconnect all 4 tubes.
  • Push a piece of filament into each load point until it comes out the back where you disconnected it. Remove any broken pieces of filament.
  • With all for PFE tubes out of the way, tilt the AMS onto its front face and gently shake it. You may be able to get broken filament out from where the tubes were connected this way. Inspect each opening for filament and remove it.
  • Remove the PTFE release button from the back of the AMS by pinching the two tabs at the top of the button.
  • Look inside the PTFE tube from the back and remove any broken filament you see.
  • Reconnect the PTFE release button and all 4 PTFE tubes
  • Connect the AMS to the printer (power + PTFE)
  • Disconnect the AMS PTFE tube from the printer (leave it connected to the back of the AMS)
  • Turn the printer on and insert the filaments one at a time. Each filament should load almost all the way to the printer end of the PTFE tube. Inspect the end of the tube for any broken pieces of filament and remove as needed.

If it still doesn't work there are additional steps at bambu labs

docs.title: Home Lab
docs.text: Notes and procedures for home lab, networking, servers, automation, and backups.

docs.title: Running it locally
docs.text:
Install venv and dependencies:

python3 -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
pip install -r requirements.txt

Run a local server:

mkdocs serve

View it in the browser at http://127.0.0.1:8000

docs.title: Deployment
docs.text: Push main to trigger a GitHub Action that builds the site and deploys it to the gh-pages branch. See .github/workflows/deploy.yml for details.

docs.title: Analytics
docs.text:
Page views are tracked with GoatCounter.

  • Site: https://dafalcon.goatcounter.com
  • Loader: docs/javascripts/analytics.js
  • MkDocs hook: extra_javascript in mkdocs.yml

The custom loader keeps tracking working with Material's navigation.instant behavior, so client-side page transitions still register as page views.
